rationale: Plain text had the lowest measured input-token cost.
Plain text preserved chunk isolation in every available controlled output.
Timestamped paragraphs had the highest overhead and the only observed cross-boundary length failure.
JSON blocks may support deterministic merge validation, but block-preserving output was not tested.

google/gemma-4-e2b.available: five independent plain-text early-chunk cold repeats

token_overhead.basis: model-formatted input token counts recorded in 27 private response artifacts

facts: Five plain-text early outputs were byte-stable, semantically complete, chunk-isolated, and exact on inspected protected values.
The outputs performed little punctuation or paragraph cleanup.
google/gemma-4-e2b.missing_evidence: No controlled timestamped or JSON representation outputs were recovered.

facts: Plain text was complete for early, middle, and late chunks.
Timestamped and JSON context each silently deleted a substantial tail from the early chunk while transport reported stop.
JSON context improved punctuation on the late chunk but did not provide a reliable cross-position advantage.

google/gemma-4-12b-qat.recommendation: plain_text or json_whisper_blocks; prefer JSON when exact protected surfaces and future block identity matter
facts: Plain and JSON outputs were complete and chunk-isolated across all three positions.
The early JSON output preserved inspected digit surfaces exactly while plain and timestamped forms preserved numeric meaning but changed surface form.
The timestamped late output exhausted 2031 output tokens, ended with finish_reason length, and crossed the requested chunk boundary.
All nine outputs remained Russian, chunk-isolated, wrapper-free, and free of runaway repetition.
Plain text provided the strongest early paragraphing.
Structured context added token cost without a stable semantic advantage, and all late outputs performed little cleanup.

position_findings.early: Largest representation-sensitive quality differences; two E4B structured-context outputs silently deleted content.
position_findings.middle: Most stable position across representations and models.
position_findings.late: Frequently copied with little cleanup; 12B timestamped context produced the only boundary and length failure.

cross_cutting.protected_value_note: Exact and semantic preservation are reported separately; 12B changed some digit surfaces while preserving numeric meaning.
cross_cutting.asr_correction_boundary: Punctuation and casing edits were reviewed, but no audio truth exists for WER/CER or uncertain technical-name corrections.

private_artifact_classes_inspected: frozen sanitized long Russian Whisper transcript
three full-context representations with bound digests
27 controlled response envelopes and final texts for E4B, 12B QAT, and 26B MoE
20 independent cold plain/early response envelopes across four models

fact_interpretation_hypothesis.fact: Structured context increased model-formatted input tokens by 27.8-29.6 percent and did not provide a general quality win in the recovered controlled outputs.
fact_interpretation_hypothesis.interpretation: Plain text is the safest default for the current plain-output cleanup contract.
fact_interpretation_hypothesis.hypothesis: JSON blocks may outperform plain text in a future block-preserving output and merge contract with exact ID/order validation.

unresolved_evidence_gaps: E2B lacks a controlled timestamped/JSON and middle/late comparison.
E4B, 12B QAT, and 26B MoE representation cells have one attempt each.
Block-preserving JSON output and merge validation were not tested.
No audio truth, WER/CER, timestamp-accuracy, or authoritative technical-name spelling check exists.
No complete manual source-unit-to-output alignment exists for all controlled cells.
Cache reuse, production concurrency, translation, and microphone behavior are outside this evidence.
